# Threat Model

> [!NOTE]
> The content below is the exported Markdown from the AWS Threat Composer.

-----

# Social Recipe
## Application Info
With Social Recipe, Users can **create, share, and view** recipes. They can engage with others by **voting and commenting** their recipes.


## Architecture
### Introduction
The entire application is running in **Kubernetes**.

Users access the application through their **web browser**. The single-page application (SPA) is build using Node.js, TypeScript, React, Tailwind, and NextUI. For user authentication, the SPA uses OAuth. Users are redirected to the auth service where they authenticate and get redirected back to the application. When the application makes API calls to the backend, it includes the users access token in the `Authorization` header.

The **Auth Service** is responsible for **user authentication**. The Open Source Identity provider [**Authentik**](https://goauthentik.io/ "Authentik") is used. Users can register and login. On success, they are redirected to the SPA.

The **business logic** is provided by the **backend service** via **REST API**. While some API endpoints are public, some others have optional or requried authentication. Access tokens are validated against the auth service. The backend is written in TypeScript using Hono and is running as Node.js server.

Data is persisted in a relational **PostgreSQL** database which is only accessible throug the backend service.
### Architecture Diagram
![Architecture Diagram](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)


## Dataflow
### Introduction
#### Web App Access

Users access the Web App via their Browser by using HTTP `GET` calls to the nginx server that serves the static assets.

#### User Authentication

If users use the "Login" button, they are redirected to the Auth Service (Authentik) where they can enter their credentials. Registration is also possible if users don't have an account. On success, users are redirected back to the Web App.

#### APIs

The web application gets its data from the backend service which provides a REST API over HTTP.

The web app can include the users access token in the `Authorization` header when making calls to the API.

All data in the request and response body is serialized as JSON.

- `/`
  - `GET` A "Hello World" Endpoint with the amount of stored `Recipe`s in the Database
- `/recipes`
  - `POST` Create a new `Recipe`
  - `GET` Get a list of all Recipes
    - Anonymous users only see public recipes
    - Signed-in users can see all public and their own private recipes
- `/recipes/:id`
  - `GET` Get details about a single `Recipe` (Only for public or own `Recipe`s)
  - `PUT` Update a `Recipe` (Owner only)
  - `DELETE` Delete a `Recipe` (Owner only)
- `/recipes/:recipeId/comments`
  - `POST` Add a new `RecipeComment` for the `Recipe` with the given `recipeId` (Logged-in users only)
  - `GET` Get all `RecipeComment`s for the `Recipe` with the given `recipeId`
- `/recipes/:recipeId/comments/:commentId`
  - `DELETE` Delete the `RecipeComment` with the given `commentId` (Logged-in users only)

#### Backend and Database

All data (`Recipe`s and `RecipeComment`s) are stored in a PostgreSQL database which is only accessible via the backend service.

The backend is responsible for providing the data to the web app via REST API and handle all business logic.

Certain interactions require an login, some endpoints can have a login (optional), and some endpoints are public. The backend is responsible for handling the authentication and authorization here. Therefore, the provided Access Token of the User from the `Authorization` header is validated against the Auth Service.
### Dataflow Diagram
![Dataflow Diagram](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)


## Assumptions


| Assumption Number | Assumption | Linked Threats | Linked Mitigations | Comments |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <a name="A-0007"></a>A-0007 | Users are aware of social engineering attacks and check the visited domain | [**T-0015**](#T-0015): A threat actor with a similar domain can trick users into using the malicious site, resulting in reduced confidentiality |  |  |
| <a name="A-0006"></a>A-0006 | The security of the Kubernetes project is properly managed \(code security, build integrity, infrastructure, etc.\). |  |  |  |
| <a name="A-0005"></a>A-0005 | The security of the nginx Ingress Controler is propererly managed and a secure version of nginx is used. |  |  |  |
| <a name="A-0004"></a>A-0004 | The devices and browsers of the users are safe. |  |  |  |
| <a name="A-0003"></a>A-0003 | The security of Authentik \(which we deploy from a Helm-Chart\) and all its components \(PostgreSQL, Redis\) is properly managed. |  |  |  |
| <a name="A-0002"></a>A-0002 | The infrastructure where the Kubernetes Cluster is running is secure \(Host OS etc.\).  |  |  |  |
| <a name="A-0001"></a>A-0001 | The Kubernetes Cluster \(provided by the Platform Team\) is secure. |  |  |  |


## Threats


| Threat Number | Threat | Mitigations | Assumptions | Status | Priority | STRIDE | Comments |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <a name="T-0016"></a>T-0016 | A threat actor can automatically create a large number of recipes, resulting in reduced availability of Social Recipe Application | [**M-0011**](#M-0011): Implement Rate Limiting |  |  Identified | Medium | D |  |
| <a name="T-0015"></a>T-0015 | A threat actor with a similar domain can trick users into using the malicious site, resulting in reduced confidentiality |  | [**A-0007**](#A-0007): Users are aware of social engineering attacks and check the visited domain |  Resolved | Low | S |  |
| <a name="T-0014"></a>T-0014 | An internal actor can add malicious code to the project, which leads to execution of the malicious code in the production environment | [**M-0008**](#M-0008): Use Code Reviews<br/>[**M-0009**](#M-0009): Use Static Application Security Testing \(SAST\)<br/>[**M-0012**](#M-0012): Use Software Component Analysis \(SCA\) |  |  Identified | High | T |  |
| <a name="T-0013"></a>T-0013 | A threat actor with knowledge about the used technologies can exploit a vulnerability in an used third-party component, resulting in reduced confidentiality, integrity and\/or availability of Social Recipe Application | [**M-0006**](#M-0006): Track used Third-Party Components<br/>[**M-0007**](#M-0007): Regularly update Third-Party Components<br/>[**M-0012**](#M-0012): Use Software Component Analysis \(SCA\) |  |  Identified | High | T |  |
| <a name="T-0012"></a>T-0012 | A threat actor sending unexpeced input to the system can receive application internal data \(e.g. exception\), which leads to insights into the system and knowledge to more efficiently attack the system, negatively impacting Intellectual Property | [**M-0005**](#M-0005): Use a global Error Handling Middleware to prevent that raw exceptions are sent to the caller |  |  Identified | Medium | I |  |
| <a name="T-0011"></a>T-0011 | An internal actor with access to the log storage system can takeover session by using logged data \(like tokens\), resulting in reduced integrity and\/or confidentiality | [**M-0004**](#M-0004): Don't log sensitive information |  |  Identified | Medium | S |  |
| <a name="T-0010"></a>T-0010 | A threat actor with access to the log storage system can takeover session by using logged data \(like tokens\), resulting in reduced integrity and\/or confidentiality | [**M-0004**](#M-0004): Don't log sensitive information |  |  Identified | Medium | S |  |
| <a name="T-0009"></a>T-0009 | A threat actor can execute arbitrary commands on the PostgreSQL database, resulting in reduced integrity, confidentiality and\/or availability of Social Recipe PostgreSQL | [**M-0002**](#M-0002): Implement input validation and sanitization \(Schema Validation\)<br/>[**M-0010**](#M-0010): Use Parameterized Queries |  |  Identified | High | R, I, D, E, T |  |
| <a name="T-0008"></a>T-0008 | A threat actor with knowledge about web development can execute code on other users devices, resulting in reduced confidentiality and\/or integrity | [**M-0002**](#M-0002): Implement input validation and sanitization \(Schema Validation\) |  |  Identified | High | T, R, I, E, S |  |
| <a name="T-0007"></a>T-0007 | A threat actor can comment on behalf of another user, resulting in reduced integrity of Comment | [**M-0001**](#M-0001): Implement proper Access Control \(Authorization\) |  |  Identified | High | R, T |  |
| <a name="T-0006"></a>T-0006 | A threat actor can modify Recipes of other users, resulting in reduced availability and\/or integrity of Recipes | [**M-0001**](#M-0001): Implement proper Access Control \(Authorization\) |  |  Identified | High | T, R |  |
| <a name="T-0005"></a>T-0005 | A threat actor can access private Recipes of another user, which leads to exposure of private data, resulting in reduced confidentiality of Recipes | [**M-0001**](#M-0001): Implement proper Access Control \(Authorization\) |  |  Identified | Medium | I |  |
| <a name="T-0004"></a>T-0004 | An internal actor who has access to the source code but no permissions for the production PostgreSQL database can get read and write access to the production PostgreSQL database, which leads to elevated privileges that exceed the internal users regular permissions, resulting in reduced integrity, confidentiality and\/or availability of Social Recipe PostgreSQL | [**M-0003**](#M-0003): Securely inject sensitive values at runtime and remove them from the codebase |  |  Identified | Medium | T, R, I, D, E |  |
| <a name="T-0003"></a>T-0003 | A threat actor who has access to the source code can get administrative credentials for the PostgreSQL database, which leads to read and write access to the production database, resulting in reduced integrity, confidentiality and\/or availability of Social Recipe PostgreSQL | [**M-0003**](#M-0003): Securely inject sensitive values at runtime and remove them from the codebase |  |  Identified | Medium | R, I, D, E, T |  |
| <a name="T-0002"></a>T-0002 | An internal actor who has access to the source code but no permissions for the auth service can get administrative credentials for the auth service, which leads to elevated privileges that exceed the internal users regular permissions, resulting in reduced integrity, confidentiality and\/or availability of auth service | [**M-0003**](#M-0003): Securely inject sensitive values at runtime and remove them from the codebase |  |  Identified | Medium | R, I, D, E, T |  |
| <a name="T-0001"></a>T-0001 | A threat actor who has access to the source code can get administrative credentials for the auth service, which leads to full control of the auth service, resulting in reduced integrity, confidentiality and\/or availability of auth service | [**M-0003**](#M-0003): Securely inject sensitive values at runtime and remove them from the codebase |  |  Identified | Medium | R, I, D, E, T |  |


## Mitigations


| Mitigation Number | Mitigation | Threats Mitigating | Assumptions | Status | Comments |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <a name="M-0012"></a>M-0012 | Use Software Component Analysis \(SCA\) | [**T-0013**](#T-0013): A threat actor with knowledge about the used technologies can exploit a vulnerability in an used third-party component, resulting in reduced confidentiality, integrity and\/or availability of Social Recipe Application<br/>[**T-0014**](#T-0014): An internal actor can add malicious code to the project, which leads to execution of the malicious code in the production environment |  | Identified |  |
| <a name="M-0011"></a>M-0011 | Implement Rate Limiting | [**T-0016**](#T-0016): A threat actor can automatically create a large number of recipes, resulting in reduced availability of Social Recipe Application |  | Identified |  |
| <a name="M-0010"></a>M-0010 | Use Parameterized Queries | [**T-0009**](#T-0009): A threat actor can execute arbitrary commands on the PostgreSQL database, resulting in reduced integrity, confidentiality and\/or availability of Social Recipe PostgreSQL |  | Identified |  |
| <a name="M-0009"></a>M-0009 | Use Static Application Security Testing \(SAST\) | [**T-0014**](#T-0014): An internal actor can add malicious code to the project, which leads to execution of the malicious code in the production environment |  | Identified |  |
| <a name="M-0008"></a>M-0008 | Use Code Reviews | [**T-0014**](#T-0014): An internal actor can add malicious code to the project, which leads to execution of the malicious code in the production environment |  | Identified |  |
| <a name="M-0007"></a>M-0007 | Regularly update Third-Party Components | [**T-0013**](#T-0013): A threat actor with knowledge about the used technologies can exploit a vulnerability in an used third-party component, resulting in reduced confidentiality, integrity and\/or availability of Social Recipe Application |  | Identified |  |
| <a name="M-0006"></a>M-0006 | Track used Third-Party Components | [**T-0013**](#T-0013): A threat actor with knowledge about the used technologies can exploit a vulnerability in an used third-party component, resulting in reduced confidentiality, integrity and\/or availability of Social Recipe Application |  | Identified |  |
| <a name="M-0005"></a>M-0005 | Use a global Error Handling Middleware to prevent that raw exceptions are sent to the caller | [**T-0012**](#T-0012): A threat actor sending unexpeced input to the system can receive application internal data \(e.g. exception\), which leads to insights into the system and knowledge to more efficiently attack the system, negatively impacting Intellectual Property |  | Identified |  |
| <a name="M-0004"></a>M-0004 | Don't log sensitive information | [**T-0010**](#T-0010): A threat actor with access to the log storage system can takeover session by using logged data \(like tokens\), resulting in reduced integrity and\/or confidentiality<br/>[**T-0011**](#T-0011): An internal actor with access to the log storage system can takeover session by using logged data \(like tokens\), resulting in reduced integrity and\/or confidentiality |  | Identified |  |
| <a name="M-0003"></a>M-0003 | Securely inject sensitive values at runtime and remove them from the codebase | [**T-0004**](#T-0004): An internal actor who has access to the source code but no permissions for the production PostgreSQL database can get read and write access to the production PostgreSQL database, which leads to elevated privileges that exceed the internal users regular permissions, resulting in reduced integrity, confidentiality and\/or availability of Social Recipe PostgreSQL<br/>[**T-0003**](#T-0003): A threat actor who has access to the source code can get administrative credentials for the PostgreSQL database, which leads to read and write access to the production database, resulting in reduced integrity, confidentiality and\/or availability of Social Recipe PostgreSQL<br/>[**T-0002**](#T-0002): An internal actor who has access to the source code but no permissions for the auth service can get administrative credentials for the auth service, which leads to elevated privileges that exceed the internal users regular permissions, resulting in reduced integrity, confidentiality and\/or availability of auth service<br/>[**T-0001**](#T-0001): A threat actor who has access to the source code can get administrative credentials for the auth service, which leads to full control of the auth service, resulting in reduced integrity, confidentiality and\/or availability of auth service |  | Identified |  |
| <a name="M-0002"></a>M-0002 | Implement input validation and sanitization \(Schema Validation\) | [**T-0008**](#T-0008): A threat actor with knowledge about web development can execute code on other users devices, resulting in reduced confidentiality and\/or integrity<br/>[**T-0009**](#T-0009): A threat actor can execute arbitrary commands on the PostgreSQL database, resulting in reduced integrity, confidentiality and\/or availability of Social Recipe PostgreSQL |  | Identified |  |
| <a name="M-0001"></a>M-0001 | Implement proper Access Control \(Authorization\) | [**T-0007**](#T-0007): A threat actor can comment on behalf of another user, resulting in reduced integrity of Comment<br/>[**T-0006**](#T-0006): A threat actor can modify Recipes of other users, resulting in reduced availability and\/or integrity of Recipes<br/>[**T-0005**](#T-0005): A threat actor can access private Recipes of another user, which leads to exposure of private data, resulting in reduced confidentiality of Recipes |  | Identified |  |


## Impacted Assets


| Assets Number | Asset | Related Threats |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AS-0001 | Social Recipe Application | [**T-0016**](#T-0016): A threat actor can automatically create a large number of recipes, resulting in reduced availability of Social Recipe Application<br/>[**T-0013**](#T-0013): A threat actor with knowledge about the used technologies can exploit a vulnerability in an used third-party component, resulting in reduced confidentiality, integrity and\/or availability of Social Recipe Application |
| AS-0002 | Intellectual Property | [**T-0012**](#T-0012): A threat actor sending unexpeced input to the system can receive application internal data \(e.g. exception\), which leads to insights into the system and knowledge to more efficiently attack the system, negatively impacting Intellectual Property |
| AS-0003 | Social Recipe PostgreSQL | [**T-0009**](#T-0009): A threat actor can execute arbitrary commands on the PostgreSQL database, resulting in reduced integrity, confidentiality and\/or availability of Social Recipe PostgreSQL<br/>[**T-0004**](#T-0004): An internal actor who has access to the source code but no permissions for the production PostgreSQL database can get read and write access to the production PostgreSQL database, which leads to elevated privileges that exceed the internal users regular permissions, resulting in reduced integrity, confidentiality and\/or availability of Social Recipe PostgreSQL<br/>[**T-0003**](#T-0003): A threat actor who has access to the source code can get administrative credentials for the PostgreSQL database, which leads to read and write access to the production database, resulting in reduced integrity, confidentiality and\/or availability of Social Recipe PostgreSQL |
| AS-0004 | Comment | [**T-0007**](#T-0007): A threat actor can comment on behalf of another user, resulting in reduced integrity of Comment |
| AS-0005 | Recipes | [**T-0006**](#T-0006): A threat actor can modify Recipes of other users, resulting in reduced availability and\/or integrity of Recipes<br/>[**T-0005**](#T-0005): A threat actor can access private Recipes of another user, which leads to exposure of private data, resulting in reduced confidentiality of Recipes |
| AS-0006 | auth service | [**T-0002**](#T-0002): An internal actor who has access to the source code but no permissions for the auth service can get administrative credentials for the auth service, which leads to elevated privileges that exceed the internal users regular permissions, resulting in reduced integrity, confidentiality and\/or availability of auth service<br/>[**T-0001**](#T-0001): A threat actor who has access to the source code can get administrative credentials for the auth service, which leads to full control of the auth service, resulting in reduced integrity, confidentiality and\/or availability of auth service |
